data.title: TreeHouse Foods Non-GAAP EPS of $0.95 misses by $0.02, revenue of $905.7M misses by $2.2M
data.description:
• TreeHouse Foods press release (NYSE:THS): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.95 misses by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $905.7M (-0.6% Y/Y) misses by $2.2M.
  • Issued initial fiscal year 2025 outlook for adjusted net sales in a range of $3.340 billion to $3.400 billion, adjusted EBITDA in a range of $345 million to $375 million, and free cash flow of at least $130 million.

data.title: European markets mostly lower, taking breather from touching record highs
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 6.45am ET.

London (UKX) -0.21% to 8,745.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.24% to 22,556, Germany’s wholesale prices rose by 0.9% Y/Y in January.

France (CAC:IND) +0.34% to 8,192

The annual inflation rate in Spain rose to 2.9% in January.

Slovakia’s economy expanded by 1.8% Y/Y in Q4.

Lithuania’s annual inflation rate accelerated to 3.6% in January.

Hungary's construction output declined by 4.2% Y/Y in December.

Switzerland’s producer and import prices fell 0.3% Y/Y in January.

Euro Area GDP expected to grow 0.1% in Q4.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.06% higher after trading lower earlier in the day. For the week so far, the STOXX 50 soared about 3% and the STOXX 50 added nearly 2%. The euro hovered around $1.04, near two-week highs, as traders weighed President Trump's trade policies and the potential end of the war in Ukraine.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.54%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.43%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.53%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK).

data.title: K2 Gold to raise C$2M via private placement
data.description:
K2 Gold (TSXV:KTO:CA) (OTCQB:KTGDF) announced a non-brokered private placement whereby the company will issue up to 13.33M units at a price of C$0.15 per share.

Total gross proceeds of up to C$2M.

Each unit will consist of one common share in the capital of the company and one half of one non-transferable common share purchase warrant.

Each warrant will be exercisable to acquire one share at a price of C$0.30 per share for a period of 12 months from the date of issuance subject to an acceleration clause.

Net proceeds will be used for exploration and to complete permitting at the Mojave project on the company's Mojave Gold project located in Inyo County, California, USA, as well as for general working capital.

data.description:
• Federal National Mortgage Association press release (OTCQB:FNMA): Q4 net income of $4.1B.
  • Revenue of $7.29B (-0.7% Y/Y).

data.title: ESS Tech CEO Eric Dresselhuys resigns, Kelly Goodman named interim chief
data.description:
ESS Tech (NYSE:GWH) announced Friday CEO Eric Dresselhuys has resigned from the role and as a member of the board of directors.

Kelly Goodman, current vice president of legal, has been appointed interim CEO. Goodman will be supported by an Office of the Interim CEO, created to lead this effort.

The Office of the Interim CEO will include Goodman, Tony Rabb, current CFO, and Ben Heng, current EVP of Engineering.

Goodman has nearly 20 years of experience in the energy legal sector both in the U.S. and internationally, supporting matters related to project development, technology agreements, and project and corporate financing.

ESS is now actively bidding on projects for daily cycling applications in the 12 to 24 hour long duration storage market – with initial installations targeted to commence in 2027 – to serve emerging AI/data center-driven load needs and to firm baseload renewable production.

The board has engaged advisors to evaluate potential commercial or financial transactions to enable this new strategic direction.

Source: Press Release

data.title: TC Energy Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.05
data.description:
• TC Energy press release (NYSE:TRP): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.05.
  • Comparable EBITDA of C$2.6 billion compared to C$2.7 billion in fourth quarter 2023

data.title: 180 Degree Capital reports NAV of $4.64 as of December 31
data.description:
180 Degree Capital (NASDAQ:TURN) Friday reported Net Asset Value Per Share of $4.64 as of December 31, 2024.

“While our full-year performance was disappointing, Q1 2025 has thus far continued and exceeded our strong performance exiting 2024," Kevin M. Rendino, Chief Executive Officer of 180 Degree Capital said.

"Our gross total return of +205% from inception through the end of 2024 continues to compare favorably to the +69% total return for the Russell Microcap Index."

data.title: Moderna GAAP EPS of -$2.91 misses by $0.15, revenue of $966M beats by $24.86M
data.description:
• Moderna press release (NASDAQ:MRNA): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$2.91 misses by $0.15.
  • Loss includes approximately $0.2 billion of non-cash charges related to manufacturing resizing.
  • Expects 2025 revenue range of $1.5 to 2.5 billion andending cash balance of approximately $6 billion
  • Revenue of $966M (-65.6% Y/Y) beats by $24.86M.

data.title: Essent Group GAAP EPS of $1.58 misses by $0.08, revenue of $315.03M beats by $1.15M
data.description:
• Essent Group press release (NYSE:ESNT): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.58 misses by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $315.03M (+6.0% Y/Y) beats by $1.15M.
  • New insurance written for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $12.2 billion, compared to $12.5 billion in the third quarter of 2024 and $8.8 billion in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Insurance in force as of December 31, 2024 was $243.6 billion, compared to $243.0 billion as of September 30, 2024 and $239.1 billion as of December 31, 2023.

data.title: MTY Food Group reports Q4 results
data.description:
• MTY Food Group press release (OTCPK:MTYFF): Q4 Net loss attributable to owners of $55.3 million, or $2.34 per diluted share
  • Revenue of $284.46M (+1.6% Y/Y).
  • Normalized adjusted EBITDA remained relatively stable at $59.4 million in the quarter, compared to $60.4 million in Q4-23.
  • System sales for the quarter improved by 2% or $30.3 million to reach $1,371.9 million compared to $1,341.6 million in Q4-23 mostly due to organic growth.
  • Ended the quarter with 7079 locations with net positive openings of 13 locations for the quarter.
